beats.label: Opening
beats.speaker: Mentor
beats.text: Nine months of savings with a family planned is not a runway for a business; it is a fuse for a stressful transition.

beats.label: Opening
beats.speaker: Sarcastic buddy
beats.text: Nine months of savings with a family on the horizon doesn't mean starting a business is easy, it means your runway is actually four.

beats.label: Opening
beats.speaker: 18-year-old you
beats.text: At thirty-four, with nine months of runway and zero kids yet, the only real data point missing is your actual guts.

beats.label: Opening
beats.speaker: Scared parents
beats.text: At thirty-four, you have nine months of savings, a partner, and family plans, but we don't know the cost of your healthcare yet.

beats.label: Rebuttal
beats.speaker: Mentor
beats.text: I disagree with our eighteen-year-old self; guts without a validated customer is just an expensive way to stress your partner before the baby arrives.

beats.label: Rebuttal
beats.speaker: Sarcastic buddy
beats.text: To 18-year-old me: Guts don't pay for diapers when that nine-month runway evaporates in four, kid; this isn't a movie, it's math.

beats.label: Rebuttal
beats.speaker: 18-year-old you
beats.text: To the Mentor: You call nine months a fuse, but the real explosion is staying comfortable while your dream dies of old age.

beats.label: Rebuttal
beats.speaker: Scared parents
beats.text: Eighteen-year-old self, guts won't pay for prenatal care or COBRA when those nine months vanish and you are starting a family.

beats.label: Verdict
beats.speaker: Council chair
beats.text: You have the capability and the drive, but executing this transition right now requires a structured bridge, not a blind leap.

verdict.decision: Launch the business, but only after securing a signed pilot customer or contract while keeping your job.
verdict.conditions: You must validate demand with one paying client and establish a strict family-approved budget before resigning.
verdict.firstMove: Draft a pitch deck and reach out to three warm leads for a pre-sales conversation tonight.

alignment.keyConcerns: Launching without validated customer demand and draining your savings prematurely.
Underestimating the real cost of family planning on a short runway.
Allowing fear and planning to delay your dream indefinitely.
Losing stable healthcare and financial security just before having a baby.
